name: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam 1990 + OIC Charter Framework
summary: The Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam 1990 (formally Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam adopted by the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation OIC) is a Pan-Islamic supranational meta-framework Islamic-context human-rights soft-law instrument adopted by the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC — formerly Organisation of the Islamic Conference 1969-2011) — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational meta-framework operating formal Islamic-context human-rights framework (Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam adopted 5 August 1990 by 19th Islamic Conference of Foreign Ministers in Cairo + soft-law framework — formal Islamic-context human-rights framework explicitly based on Islamic Sharia framework via Article 24 + Article 25 framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks which operate on secular human-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational human-rights framework with formal foundational Islamic Sharia framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal OIC Pan-Islamic human-rights elaboration framework (Cairo Declaration adopted as Islamic supplement to Universal Declaration of Human Rights 1948 framework — applicable to all 57 OIC member states as soft-law framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Pan-Islamic human-rights soft-law framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Cairo Declaration Article 5 family-rights framework (Cairo Declaration Article 5 framework on family-rights — formal Islamic-context family-rights framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks operating without formal Islamic-context family-rights framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern Islamic-context family-rights formal framework), and as the central jurisdiction of one of the most distinctive modern soft-law-vs-binding-treaty framework debates globally (Cairo Declaration soft-law framework + 2008 OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission IPHRC framework + 2020 Cairo Declaration Update Process framework — formal ongoing OIC framework debate on transitioning from soft-law to binding-treaty framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-human-rights frameworks operating on either binding-treaty framework or soft-law framework without transition framework). The Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam 1990 is binding upon 57 OIC member states as soft-law framework — universal Pan-Islamic coverage framework as soft-law framework. The Declaration is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 5 family-rights framework intersects with parental-rights framework. The OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission IPHRC is the formal monitoring framework body; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam 1990
statutory_framework.title: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam
statutory_framework.relevance: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam adopted 5 August 1990 by 19th Islamic Conference of Foreign Ministers in Cairo — formal Islamic-context human-rights soft-law framework explicitly based on Islamic Sharia framework.

statutory_framework.title: Cairo Declaration Article 5
statutory_framework.relevance: Cairo Declaration Article 5 framework on family-rights — formal Islamic-context family-rights framework.

statutory_framework.title: Cairo Declaration Articles 24 + 25
statutory_framework.relevance: Cairo Declaration Article 24 framework providing all rights specified in the Declaration are subject to Islamic Sharia + Article 25 framework providing Islamic Sharia as the only source of reference for explanation or clarification of any Articles — formal foundational Islamic Sharia framework underlying Cairo Declaration.

statutory_framework.citation: OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission IPHRC 2008
statutory_framework.title: OIC IPHRC
statutory_framework.relevance: OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission IPHRC established 2008 under OIC Charter 2008 framework — formal OIC monitoring framework body.

statutory_framework.title: OIC Charter
statutory_framework.relevance: Organisation of Islamic Cooperation OIC Charter adopted 1972 + revised 2008 — formal Pan-Islamic regional supranational framework underlying Cairo Declaration.

apex_courts.name: OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission (IPHRC)
apex_courts.role: Formal monitoring framework body for Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam.

professional_regulators.name: OIC Secretariat
professional_regulators.role: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: Cairo Declaration-related individual communications are not formally accepted under OIC IPHRC framework — soft-law cooperative framework.

key_developments.title: OIC founded
key_developments.description: Organisation of the Islamic Conference OIC founded 25 September 1969 in Rabat following Al-Aqsa Mosque arson attack 21 August 1969 — beginning of formal Pan-Islamic regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: OIC Charter adopted
key_developments.description: OIC Charter adopted 1972 — formal Pan-Islamic regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: Cairo Declaration adopted
key_developments.description: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam adopted 5 August 1990 by 19th Islamic Conference of Foreign Ministers in Cairo — formal Islamic-context human-rights soft-law framework.

key_developments.title: OIC Charter revision + IPHRC
key_developments.description: OIC Charter revised 2008 + OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission IPHRC established 2008 — formal OIC monitoring framework body.

key_developments.title: OIC renamed Organisation of Islamic Cooperation
key_developments.description: Organisation of Islamic Cooperation renamed from Organisation of the Islamic Conference 28 June 2011 — formal Pan-Islamic regional supranational framework renaming.

key_developments.title: Cairo Declaration Update Process
key_developments.description: Cairo Declaration Update Process commenced 2020 — formal ongoing OIC framework debate on transitioning from soft-law to binding-treaty framework.

structural_findings: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam operates a supranational Pan-Islamic Islamic-context human-rights soft-law framework — places Cairo Declaration in the Pan-Islamic supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern supranational meta-framework operating formal Islamic-context human-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal Islamic-context human-rights framework explicitly based on Islamic Sharia framework.
Central jurisdiction of formal OIC Pan-Islamic human-rights elaboration framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Pan-Islamic human-rights soft-law framework.
Central jurisdiction of formal Cairo Declaration Article 5 family-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Islamic-context family-rights formal framework.
Central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern soft-law-vs-binding-treaty framework debates globally is structurally distinctive globally — formal ongoing OIC framework debate.
57 OIC member state soft-law framework applicability is structurally distinctive globally — universal Pan-Islamic coverage framework as soft-law framework.

1969 OIC + 1972 OIC Charter + 1990 Cairo Declaration + 2008 OIC Charter revision + IPHRC + 2011 renaming + 2020 Update Process Islamic-human-rights-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

Cairo Declaration Articles 24 + 25 formal foundational Islamic Sharia framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational human-rights framework with formal foundational Islamic Sharia framework.

sources.title: OIC Independent Permanent Human Rights Commission (IPHRC)

editorial_notes: Cairo Declaration on Human Rights in Islam jurisdiction sidecar — supranational Pan-Islamic Islamic-context human-rights soft-law meta-framework (Cairo Declaration 1990 + Article 5 Family Rights + Articles 24 + 25 Sharia Framework + OIC Charter 1972/2008 + OIC IPHRC 2008 + OIC founded 1969 + OIC renaming 2011 + Cairo Declaration Update Process 2020). Only modern supranational meta-framework operating formal Islamic-context human-rights framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal OIC Pan-Islamic human-rights elaboration framework + central jurisdiction of formal Cairo Declaration Article 5 family-rights framework + central jurisdiction of one of most distinctive modern soft-law-vs-binding-treaty framework debates globally + 57 OIC member state soft-law framework applicability + 1969 OIC + 1972 OIC Charter + 1990 Cairo Declaration + 2008 OIC Charter revision + IPHRC + 2011 renaming + 2020 Update Process Islamic-human-rights-framework-development sequence + Cairo Declaration Articles 24 + 25 formal foundational Islamic Sharia framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.
